commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: Human review is needed because the external PR lacks real behavior proof and still has runtime recovery plus security hardening blockers that should be aligned with current restart-recovery architecture.; Needs attention: the prompt-trust issue appears addressed, but the new pending-inbound store still has corrupt-file read and backup-write hardening gaps.; Review finding: [P1] Persist inbound messages before replay... (sha=34f75d82c479c7d74eae78971c7215ae52da82b1)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a file-backed pending-inbound and active-turn store, startup replay/recovery hooks, embedded-runner lifecycle writes, a system-event queue export, command-queue drain-state export, tests, and a changelog entry.

Reproducibility: Do we have a high-confidence way to reproduce the issue? Yes at source level: the latest diff has no production writePendingInbound caller, active-turn recovery only queues a system event, and the store reads/backups corrupt state without byte/path hardening.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the external PR lacks real behavior proof and still has runtime recovery plus security hardening blockers that should be aligned with current restart-recovery architecture.

target.title: feat(gateway): pending-inbound store and active-turn tracking for lossless restarts
target.author: amittell

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
